prose:
Frontier-orbitals task on furan computed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level, tier 'standard' (density fitting on by default), in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral closed-shell singlet). Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz (9 atoms) used as-is — single point, not re-optimized. SCF converged in 9 cycles; engine integrity status 'ok', trustworthy=true.

Results (eV): HOMO = -6.3597638, LUMO = -0.1024036, HOMO-LUMO gap (headline) = 6.2573602 eV. Other orbitals: HOMO-1 = -7.8707441, HOMO-2 = -10.332024, LUMO+1 = 1.4483308, LUMO+2 = 1.702378. Koopmans descriptors (eV): vertical IP = 6.3597638, vertical EA = 0.1024036, electronegativity = 3.2310837, chemical hardness = 3.1286801, electrophilicity index = 1.6684195; chemical softness = 0.3196236 eV^-1. Total electronic energy = -230.11429 hartree (-6261.7289 eV); this energy zero is method-specific and only comparable to other B3LYP/def2-TZVP values.

The gap is a Koopmans-style orbital-energy gap, not an optical excitation energy. No warnings were reported by the engine (empty warnings array — clean run). Command: cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard furan.xyz. Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptv50ies2/live.json. No experimental comparison offered.
